functional_purpose: Receives DICOM studies and submits them to the OpenEyes PayloadProcessor as dicom_request work. Eligible Zeiss IOLMaster objects are also written to the IolMasterImport incoming directory.
business_process: Clinical imaging ingestion and biometry import.

destination_connectors.name: Write out file to IolMasterImport incoming folder when device is a Zeiss IOLMaster

ack_behaviour: DICOM response is auto-generated after the source transformer and processing completes.
auth_method: HTTP Basic preemptive for PayloadProcessor; file destination credentials are redacted
main_filters: Source transformer removes the IolMasterImport destination unless manufacturer contains Zeiss, model contains iolmaster, and SOP Class UID is supported
main_transformer_stages: Extract patient, study, device, and AE-title DICOM tags
Derive identifier_type and institution_id from local AET with configurationMap defaultPatientIdentifierType fallback
Set source_platform from AET
Conditionally retain IOLMasterImport destination
error_handling: No custom error handler; both destinations have queueing disabled.
queue_behaviour: Source queue buffer is 1000; both destinations have queueEnabled false.
logging: No custom logging. Production message storage retains DICOM attachments and encryption is disabled, creating a PII risk.

evidence.claim: EK DICOM listens with a DICOM Listener on port 11112 and its source transformer extracts DICOM tags.
evidence.location: EK | DICOM | da67d2ba-db52-4f50-898b-b534d71450ab | EK/DICOM.xml | sourceConnector listener and transformer steps 0-13

evidence.claim: The PayloadProcessor HTTP destination posts DICOM data to /api/v1/request/queue/add with request_type dicom_request using preemptive Basic authentication.
evidence.location: EK | DICOM | da67d2ba-db52-4f50-898b-b534d71450ab | EK/DICOM.xml | destination 'PayloadProcessor API Send' properties

evidence.claim: The IolMasterImport File Writer is removed unless the source transformer identifies a supported Zeiss IOLMaster object.
evidence.location: EK | DICOM | da67d2ba-db52-4f50-898b-b534d71450ab | EK/DICOM.xml | source transformer 'Remove the IOLMaster Import destination if device is not a Zeiss IOL Master'

functional_purpose: Polls a local correspondence drop folder for PDF and XML files, then copies the raw file to an SMB share. Successfully read source files are deleted.
business_process: Operational distribution of correspondence documents to a file share.
main_filters: File Reader accepts *.pdf and *.xml; no Mirth filter rules
main_transformer_stages: Pass raw file content unchanged to File Writer
error_handling: Source read errors are moved to the configured error directory.
queue_behaviour: Source queue buffer is 1000; destination queueEnabled is false.
logging: No custom logging. Production message content is retained unencrypted, creating a document PII risk.

evidence.claim: EK Filedrop Correspondence is a File Reader for /mnt/docman/filedrop accepting PDF and XML, deleting successfully processed files and moving error responses to its error directory.
evidence.location: EK | Filedrop Correspondence | 7a04b9d1-70ac-4008-b809-732b88267f5c | EK/Filedrop Correspondence.xml | sourceConnector File Reader properties

evidence.claim: Its sole destination is the File Writer 'Copy file to share' using an SMB scheme and raw message content.
evidence.location: EK | Filedrop Correspondence | 7a04b9d1-70ac-4008-b809-732b88267f5c | EK/Filedrop Correspondence.xml | destination 'Copy file to share' properties and transformer

functional_purpose: Polls a local correspondence drop folder for PDF or XML files and copies each raw file to an SFTP destination. Successfully read source files are deleted.
business_process: Operational distribution of correspondence documents to an external file-transfer destination.
auth_method: SFTP key authentication; username and credentials are redacted
main_filters: File Reader accepts files matching .*\.(pdf|xml); no Mirth filter rules
main_transformer_stages: Pass raw file content unchanged to File Writer
error_handling: Source read errors are moved to the configured error directory.
queue_behaviour: Source queue buffer is 1000; destination queueEnabled is false.
logging: No custom logging. Development message content is retained unencrypted, creating a document PII risk.

evidence.claim: EK MTW Correspondence is a File Reader for /mnt/docman/mtw_filedrop, filters PDF or XML files, deletes successful source files, and moves source errors to its error directory.
evidence.location: EK | MTW Correspondence | 3ad2f609-8045-4da9-b708-2e291f18af15 | EK/MTW Correspondence.xml | sourceConnector File Reader properties

evidence.claim: Its only destination is the File Writer 'Copy file to share' using SFTP with key authentication and raw message content.
evidence.location: EK | MTW Correspondence | 3ad2f609-8045-4da9-b708-2e291f18af15 | EK/MTW Correspondence.xml | destination 'Copy file to share' properties and transformer

functional_purpose: Receives HL7 ADT messages over MLLP, transforms patient data for PASAPI V2, and updates patient and appointment records. Appointment PUT applies to A01, A05, and A08; appointment DELETE applies to A11 and A38.
business_process: PAS patient demographic and appointment synchronisation into OpenEyes.
ack_behaviour: Auto-generates HL7 AA, AE, or AR before processing.
main_filters: Clinic List PUT accepts A01, A05, or A08
Clinic List DELETE accepts A11 or A38
main_transformer_stages: XSLT transforms ADT into PASAPI patient XML
Extract HospitalNumber, ClinicCode, and VisitID
Derive visit date, visit time, A31 update-only flag, and visit-status XML
Inspect PASAPI response warnings and capture PatientId
error_handling: Response transformer marks PASAPI responses containing warnings as ERROR; transformer scripts log exceptions.
queue_behaviour: Source queue buffer is 1000; all HTTP destinations have queueEnabled false.
logging: Transformer logs PASAPI response XML and warnings, which can expose patient data.

evidence.claim: EK PAS IN Maidstone is a TCP Listener on port 6671 using HL7v2 and sends patient and appointment updates to PASAPI/V2 endpoints over Basic-authenticated HTTP.
evidence.location: EK | PAS IN Maidstone | 712ec2e2-92a4-4f7a-bc3d-b216faeddc39 | EK/PAS IN Maidstone.xml | sourceConnector listener; destinations 'PASAPI - Patient', 'Clinic List - PUT', and 'Clinic List - DELETE'

evidence.claim: Appointment PUT is filtered to A01/A05/A08 and DELETE to A11/A38; the patient transformer treats A31 as update-only.
evidence.location: EK | PAS IN Maidstone | 712ec2e2-92a4-4f7a-bc3d-b216faeddc39 | EK/PAS IN Maidstone.xml | destination filters and transformer 'UpdateOnly'

evidence.claim: The similarly structured PAS IN channel is not assumed equivalent: this channel's concrete source listener port is 6671, while EK PAS IN uses 6661.
evidence.location: EK | PAS IN Maidstone | 712ec2e2-92a4-4f7a-bc3d-b216faeddc39 | EK/PAS IN Maidstone.xml | sourceConnector listener port

evidence.claim: Appointment PUT is filtered to A01/A05/A08 and DELETE to A11/A38; the patient transformer treats A31 as update-only.

functional_purpose: Accepts HTTP patient-search parameters, creates an HL7 QBP^Q21 query, and sends it to a remote PAS over MLLP. It converts the returned K21 response to XML and returns a PatientList response after checking the message control ID.
business_process: Patient demographic query against a remote PAS.
source_system: OpenEyes or another HTTP patient-search client
ack_behaviour: Returns the transformed PatientList HTTP response only when the remote K21 MSH.10 control ID matches the sent message ID; otherwise removes the response destination.
main_filters: Convert K21 to XML only permits the final response when remote MSH.10 matches the sent connector message ID
main_transformer_stages: Create QBP query timestamp and control IDs
Populate QPD search parameters from hosnum, nhsnum, givenname, and familyname
Send Q21 over MLLP
Convert K21 response to XML
XSLT transforms K21 patient data to PatientList
error_handling: Logs a control-ID mismatch and suppresses the Create response destination.
retry_behaviour: All destinations have retryCount 0; the active MLLP sender has MLLP maxRetries 2.
queue_behaviour: Source queue buffer is 1000; all destinations have queueEnabled false.
logging: Logs a message-control-ID mismatch; no custom patient-content logging is configured.

evidence.claim: EK PAS OUT is an unauthenticated HTTP Listener on port 6663 whose source transformer builds an HL7v2 QBP^Q21 query from HTTP parameters.
evidence.location: EK | PAS OUT | 06f0b8b8-5ea0-41a4-a0bc-04b89fb5193c | EK/PAS OUT.xml | sourceConnector HTTP Listener and transformer steps 0-5

evidence.claim: The enabled 'Send Q21' TCP Sender uses MLLP, then JavaScript destinations convert its response to K21 XML and XSLT produces the response.
evidence.location: EK | PAS OUT | 06f0b8b8-5ea0-41a4-a0bc-04b89fb5193c | EK/PAS OUT.xml | destinations 'Send Q21', 'Convert K21 to XML', and 'Create response'

evidence.claim: The K21 conversion filter checks remote MSH.10 against connectorMessage message ID and removes 'Create response' on mismatch.
evidence.location: EK | PAS OUT | 06f0b8b8-5ea0-41a4-a0bc-04b89fb5193c | EK/PAS OUT.xml | destination 'Convert K21 to XML' JavaScript filter
